question.content: 3. (60 points) Rolling of a homogeneous rigid thin ring. A homogeneous rigid thin ring has radius $R$ and mass $M$, and rolls on a horizontal ground plane (the $x$-$y$ coordinate plane). Assume the friction coefficient is large enough so that there is no slipping between the ring and the ground at all times. At time $t$, let the angle between the ring plane and the vertical direction (the $z$ direction) be $\theta(t)$, and let the angle between the line of intersection of the ring plane with the ground and the $x$ direction be $\phi(t)$. The instantaneous contact point between the ring and the ground has Cartesian coordinates $(x(t), y(t), 0)$, as shown in Fig. 3a. The gravitational acceleration magnitude is $g$. Suppose the ring performs uniform circular motion: the contact point traces a circle of radius $r$ around the $z$ axis at constant speed, with $\theta(t) = \theta_0$ (a constant), and $\phi(t)$ a linear function of time $t$. In the laboratory frame $\Sigma$, without loss of generality, set $$ \begin{pmatrix} x(t) \\ y(t) \end{pmatrix} = r \begin{pmatrix} -\sin(\omega t) \\ \cos(\omega t) \end{pmatrix} $$ where $\omega$ is the angular speed to be determined. In the rotating frame $\Sigma'$ that rotates uniformly about the $z$ axis with angular speed $\omega$, both $\theta$ and $\phi$ are constants, and the ring undergoes fixed-axis rotation about its central axis perpendicular to the ring plane. In frame $\Sigma'$, each mass element of the ring experiences centrifugal and Coriolis forces. Treat the ring as a collection of infinitesimal elements, and find the net torque about the ring's center of mass due to the centrifugal force, $\boldsymbol{\tau}_{\text{centrifugal}}$, and due to the Coriolis force, $\boldsymbol{\tau}_{\text{Coriolis}}$ (the result may contain the still-undetermined $\omega$). Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Define ring axis n and rotating-frame angular velocity Omega.

step_3.content: Centrifugal-force torque uses tau_c = Omega x (I*Omega).

step_5.content: Coriolis-force torque uses no-slip spin omega_s = -r*omega/R and Fcor = -2m(Omega x v).

step_7.content: Combine two collinear torque components in the ezxn direction.
step_7.key_point: Keep final result as one compact expression.
